lead: A 70-second Insta360 360° walkthrough of the HSR office (typical floor) was reconstructed in 3D — 307 of 318 perspective views solved at 1.05 px reprojection error — and registered to the architectural plan — with the walk's start hard-anchored to the lift-lobby entry door you marked. In every area the camera actually entered, the built shell conforms to the design. The audit's value is in what it flags: one critical coverage gap, two life-safety items to physically verify, and a short list of finishing and electrical snags. Refuted claims were removed by an adversarial verification pass.
callout: Critical coverage gap — plumbing cannot be signed off. The walkthrough never entered the men's/women's toilets, the pantry, or the lift/stair core, so none of the four drawn plumbing fixtures (2 WCs, 2 wash-basins, pantry sink) were filmed. A dedicated wet-area pass is required before any plumbing or wet-core verification.
limitations: The walk's START is now pinned exactly to the user-marked entry door, which fixes orientation and scale; placement elsewhere is interpolated from semantic anchors (±~1 m near the route, looser far from it). Monocular SfM is scale-recovered from the plan, not surveyed — a screening tool, not a measured site survey.
This Mac has no CUDA GPU, so reconstruction is sparse (camera path + point cloud) — a dense watertight mesh and true wall-by-wall millimetre deviation were not computed.

The supplied plan is architectural and carries NO electrical/MEP layer, so electrical findings are judged as as-built condition vs design intent (concealed services) and safety/NBC code — not plan-position mismatches.
Toilets, pantry and the vertical-transport core were never filmed — those disciplines are UNVERIFIED, not 'passed'.
Guard-rail heights and possible hairline cracks cannot be measured from 360 imagery (barrel distortion near floor/ceiling) — they are flagged for physical check, not asserted as defects.

Every candidate finding was adversarially re-checked against the source frames; refuted items (e.g. an 'exposed concrete beam' that is actually a finished ceiling, and a suspected 'water stain') were dropped.
